headline: Only 12% of government data portals actually function as open data
context: An audit of 28 federal government open data portals found that only 12% meet international open data standards with machine-readable, downloadable, and regularly updated datasets.

cards.big: Only 12% of government 'open data' portals actually provide usable open data
cards.sub: 88% of portals offer PDFs, broken links, or data last updated in 2021

cards.big: Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation mandated open data compliance by 2023
cards.sub: Deadline passed with no consequences for non-compliant agencies

cards.big: Open data in South Korea generates estimated USD8 billion in annual economic value
cards.sub: Malaysia's non-functional portals represent massive lost economic opportunity

cards.big: MAMPU oversees data.gov.my but has no enforcement power over publishing agencies
cards.sub: Central coordination without authority creates a portal of empty shelves

cards.big: Open data that can't be opened isn't transparency — it's a website pretending to be accountability
Should agency KPIs include measurable open data publication and quality standards?
